extraction.one_line_summary: DENNIS Rohan won the Stage 1 TTT by 6 seconds over KWIATKOWSKI, building advantage throughout rather than just holding early gains.
tactical_patterns: DENNIS extended lead progressively through course rather than defending early split, suggesting pacing strategy favored back-end power.
KWIATKOWSKI third-week Tour fatigue concerns proved unfounded, nearly matching fresh specialists despite domestique duties at France.
CAMPENAERTS as European TT champion matched KWIATKOWSKI within split seconds, revealing BMC time trial depth beyond expectations.

rider_signals.PORTE Richie: negative: illness significantly compromised performance, finished far below expected GC position for declared team leader
rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: positive: BMC granted GC leadership status, confirmed by winning performance and tactical freedom previously unavailable
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michal: positive: Tour de France domestique work created no residual fatigue, contested podium against fresh specialists
rider_signals.VAN BAAL Dylan: positive: only sub-10-minute rider initially, benchmark performance suggests underrated TT capability for GC support
rider_signals.CAMPENAERTS Victor: positive: European champion credentials validated by near-podium, BMC time trial roster deeper than pre-race assessment
